# `ecma48-syntax` sequences

<!-- Generated by `qdb generate reference` from `db/ecma48-syntax.toml`. Do not edit by hand. -->

## C0 BEL (`ecma48.c0.bel`)

Preserved as a control token; semantic handling is application-specific.

- Direction: bidirectional
- Syntax: `BEL`
- References: [ECMA-48: Control Functions for Coded Character Sets, 5th edition](https://www.ecma-international.org/publications-and-standards/standards/ecma-48/) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/ecma48/c0_bel.seq`](../../../fixtures/ecma48/c0_bel.seq)

## C0 LF (`ecma48.c0.lf`)

The event decoder maps LF/CR to an Enter key for application input.

- Direction: bidirectional
- Syntax: `LF`
- References: [ECMA-48: Control Functions for Coded Character Sets, 5th edition](https://www.ecma-international.org/publications-and-standards/standards/ecma-48/) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/ecma48/c0_lf.seq`](../../../fixtures/ecma48/c0_lf.seq)

## C1 CSI (`ecma48.c1.csi_8bit`)

8-bit CSI is parsed into the same syntax token as ESC [ CSI.

- Direction: bidirectional
- Syntax: `0x9B params final`
- References: [ECMA-48: Control Functions for Coded Character Sets, 5th edition](https://www.ecma-international.org/publications-and-standards/standards/ecma-48/) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/ecma48/c1_csi.seq`](../../../fixtures/ecma48/c1_csi.seq)

## DEC Save Cursor (`ecma48.esc.dec_save_cursor`)

Legacy DEC save-cursor command; SCOSC may be preferable in some terminals.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC 7`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/ecma48/esc_dec_save_cursor.seq`](../../../fixtures/ecma48/esc_dec_save_cursor.seq)

## DEC Restore Cursor (`ecma48.esc.dec_restore_cursor`)

Legacy DEC restore-cursor command; SCORC may be preferable in some terminals.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC 8`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/ecma48/esc_dec_restore_cursor.seq`](../../../fixtures/ecma48/esc_dec_restore_cursor.seq)

## Select G0 US ASCII (`ecma48.esc.charset_g0_us_ascii`)

Charset selection is syntax-preserved but not modeled semantically.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC ( B`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/ecma48/esc_charset_g0_us_ascii.seq`](../../../fixtures/ecma48/esc_charset_g0_us_ascii.seq)

## DCS DECRQSS (`ecma48.dcs.decrqss`)

DCS payloads are preserved losslessly for extension decoders.

- Direction: bidirectional
- Syntax: `DCS payload ST`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/ecma48/negative/dcs_unterminated.seq`](../../../fixtures/ecma48/negative/dcs_unterminated.seq)

## APC Payload (`ecma48.apc.payload`)

APC is preserved as a control string; no application meaning is assigned.

- Direction: bidirectional
- Syntax: `APC payload ST`
- References: [ECMA-48: Control Functions for Coded Character Sets, 5th edition](https://www.ecma-international.org/publications-and-standards/standards/ecma-48/) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/ecma48/apc_payload.seq`](../../../fixtures/ecma48/apc_payload.seq), [`fixtures/ecma48/negative/apc_unterminated.seq`](../../../fixtures/ecma48/negative/apc_unterminated.seq)

## PM Payload (`ecma48.pm.payload`)

PM is preserved as a control string; no application meaning is assigned.

- Direction: bidirectional
- Syntax: `PM payload ST`
- References: [ECMA-48: Control Functions for Coded Character Sets, 5th edition](https://www.ecma-international.org/publications-and-standards/standards/ecma-48/) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/ecma48/pm_payload.seq`](../../../fixtures/ecma48/pm_payload.seq), [`fixtures/ecma48/negative/pm_unterminated.seq`](../../../fixtures/ecma48/negative/pm_unterminated.seq)

## SOS Payload (`ecma48.sos.payload`)

SOS is preserved as a control string; no application meaning is assigned.

- Direction: bidirectional
- Syntax: `SOS payload ST`
- References: [ECMA-48: Control Functions for Coded Character Sets, 5th edition](https://www.ecma-international.org/publications-and-standards/standards/ecma-48/) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
